bull.b: Data center now represents the majority of revenue with multi-year growth guidance (~50% FY27). Durable design wins and large customer programs in hyperscale/cloud create multi-year revenue visibility and higher ASPs, anchoring margins and enabling scale in core markets.

A diversified tech roadmap across switching, optics, DSPs and photonics reduces single-product risk and aligns with industry transitions (800G→1.6T). Multi-node capabilities and proprietary optics/IP deepen competitive moats, supporting sustained share gains in high-growth interconnect markets.

Robust operating cash flow and free cash flow alongside low relative leverage provide financial flexibility to fund supplier prepayments, R&D, M&A and capacity locking. This cash strength supports execution of multi-year ramps without immediate funding stress.

bear.b: A material portion of projected revenue and custom program upside requires sustained high cloud and AI CapEx over several years. If hyperscaler spending moderates, multiyear targets and margin expansion could be materially delayed, exposing revenue and margin sensitivity.

bear.t: Execution And Timing Risk On Ambitious Ramps
bear.b: Large initiatives (scale-up optics, XPU/custom programs, switching scale) demand multi-year engineering, supply and customer validation. Slower-than-expected ramps would postpone high-margin volume, compress near-term ROI on acquisitions and strain iterative investment plans.

Recent profitability rebound follows years of losses, indicating cyclicality and sensitivity to product cycles. Sustaining elevated margins depends on continued product mix, high-volume ramps and customer spending; reversion would pressure returns and cash conversion.
